title: vLLM 0.11.2 MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor API removal on Ascend NPU — replace with model.get_mrope_input_positions()
symptom: AttributeError: type object 'MRotaryEmbedding' has no attribute 'get_input_positions_tensor' — vLLM 0.11.2 removed this static method. The vllm_ascend NPUModelRunner._init_mrope_positions() still calls the deprecated API, causing runtime errors when serving multimodal Vision-Language Models (VLLMs) like Qwen2-VL, Qwen2.5-VL, or MinerU2.5-Pro on Ascend NPU.
root_cause: vLLM 0.11.2 restructured M-RoPE position initialization: the static method MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor(prompt_token_ids, hf_config=..., image_grid_thw=..., video_grid_thw=..., second_per_grid_ts=..., audio_feature_lengths=..., use_audio_in_video=...) was removed and replaced by the instance method model.get_mrope_input_positions(prompt_token_ids, mm_features) that consumes multimodal features directly. The vllm_ascend plugin (model_runner_v1.py) retained the old static-method call with manual mm_feature field extraction, making it incompatible with vLLM ≥0.11.2. The upstream reference implementation is at vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py lines 955-964.
fix_steps: 1. Detect the error signature: AttributeError on MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor in vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py:_init_mrope_positions during multimodal VLM serving on Ascend NPU.
2. Open the upstream reference: vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py lines 955-964 — observe the concise 8-line pattern using model.get_mrope_input_positions() directly on req_state fields.
3. Add supports_mrope to the import from vllm.model_executor.models.interfaces in model_runner_v1.py (used in the assert guard).

4. Replace the _init_mrope_positions method (remove the obsolete mm_feature field extraction loop that manually unpacks image_grid_thw, video_grid_thw, second_per_grid_ts, audio_feature_lengths, use_audio_in_video from mm_features, and remove the static MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor() call with its long keyword-argument list). Replace the entire method with: get the model via self.get_model(), assert supports_mrope(model), then call model.get_mrope_input_positions(req_state.prompt_token_ids, req_state.mm_features) and unpack the returned tuple into req_state.mrope_positions and req_state.mrope_position_delta.

5. Retain the MRotaryEmbedding import — do NOT remove it — because get_next_input_positions_tensor() is still used elsewhere in the file (line ~944) for on-the-fly completion mrope position computation.
6. Verify by running the vLLM serving entry script with a multimodal VLM model — confirm no AttributeError on M-RoPE position initialization and verify serving health-check passes.
affected_patterns: MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor(prompt_token_ids, hf_config=..., image_grid_thw=..., video_grid_thw=..., second_per_grid_ts=..., audio_feature_lengths=..., use_audio_in_video=...)
def _init_mrope_positions(self, req_state: CachedRequestState): ... # with mm_feature extraction loop and static method call

code_changes.before:
def _init_mrope_positions(self, req_state: CachedRequestState):
        # Extract mm_feature fields for the static method call
        hf_config = self.vllm_config.model_config.hf_config
        mm_features = req_state.mm_features
        image_grid_thw = []
        video_grid_thw = []
        second_per_grid_ts = []
        audio_feature_lengths = []
        use_audio_in_video = []
        for feature in mm_features:
            if isinstance(feature, dict):
                image_grid_thw.extend(feature.get('image_grid_thw', []))
                video_grid_thw.extend(feature.get('video_grid_thw', []))
                second_per_grid_ts.extend(feature.get('second_per_grid_ts', []))
                audio_feature_lengths.extend(feature.get('audio_feature_lengths', []))
                use_audio_in_video.extend(feature.get('use_audio_in_video', []))
        req_state.mrope_positions, req_state.mrope_position_delta = \
            MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor(
                req_state.prompt_token_ids,
                hf_config=hf_config,
                image_grid_thw=image_grid_thw,
                video_grid_thw=video_grid_thw,
                second_per_grid_ts=second_per_grid_ts,
                audio_feature_lengths=audio_feature_lengths,
                use_audio_in_video=use_audio_in_video,
            )

references: Upstream reference: vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py lines 955-964 — GPUModelRunner._init_mrope_positions() using model.get_mrope_input_positions()
vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py line 944 — MRotaryEmbedding.get_next_input_positions_tensor() still used for completion on-the-fly mrope computation; do NOT remove this import
antipatterns: Do NOT blindly remove the MRotaryEmbedding import from model_runner_v1.py — get_next_input_positions_tensor() is still actively used at line ~944 for completion mrope position computation.
Do NOT attempt to downgrade vLLM to restore the old API — vLLM 0.11.2 is the target version and API migration is the correct path.
Do NOT manually unpack mm_features into image_grid_thw/video_grid_thw/etc. — the new get_mrope_input_positions() consumes mm_features directly and handles all modalities internally.

meta.refiner_reasoning: This is a deterministic vLLM 0.11.2 API migration fix with a precise upstream reference pattern (gpu_model_runner.py:955-964). The fix was validated in 2 iterations: iteration 1 applied the code change, iteration 2 confirmed no mrope/rotary errors remained (the remaining UnboundLocalError in EngineCore tracer is a separate NPU engine-level issue). The 8-line replacement eliminates both the deprecated static method call and the obsolete mm_feature field extraction loop. Confidence reflects the version-specific scope — the fix applies precisely to vLLM 0.11.2→vllm_ascend compatibility but generalizes to any multimodal VLM serving on Ascend NPU when this error signature appears.

trigger_fingerprint: vLLM serving OOM on Ascend NPU with KV cache allocation failure, torch_npu.npu.memory_stats reporting significantly less allocated memory than torch_npu.npu.mem_get_info total minus free, non_torch_allocations exceeding 30% of total NPU memory, or gpu_memory_utilization setting silently violated due to invisible CANN driver allocations.

classifier.reasoning: The CANN driver memory invisibility is a fundamental architectural characteristic of the Ascend NPU runtime, not specific to any model or workload. The fix strategy (discrepancy detection between torch_npu.npu.memory_stats and torch_npu.npu.mem_get_info, profiling fallback when non-torch allocations exceed 30% threshold, and free-based safety cap at current_free * 0.9) constitutes an actionable, repeatable diagnostic-and-repair procedure. This warrants classification as a skill because it prescribes a concrete sequence of detection and mitigation steps that a fix_code or code_adapter agent can follow, rather than being a static knowledge narrative (document), a mechanical AST-level transformation (rule), or a prompt-tuning suggestion (prompt). The high priority and 0.9 confidence reflect the universal applicability to any vLLM deployment on Ascend NPU.
classifier.trigger_fingerprint: vLLM serving OOM on Ascend NPU with KV cache allocation failure, torch_npu.npu.memory_stats reporting significantly less allocated memory than torch_npu.npu.mem_get_info total minus free, non_torch_allocations exceeding 30% of total NPU memory, or gpu_memory_utilization setting silently violated due to invisible CANN driver allocations.

problem_description: On Ascend NPU builds of vLLM, the SchedulerOutput object may not expose the grammar_bitmask attribute that exists on CUDA builds. Direct attribute access (scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask) raises AttributeError and crashes the serving engine. This affects lines 2018 and 1709 in vllm_ascend's model_runner_v1.py.
rough_fix_approach: 1) Wrap all accesses to scheduler_output.grammar_bitmask with hasattr/getattr guards: `getattr(scheduler_output, 'grammar_bitmask', None)`. 2) When grammar_bitmask is None, skip or provide default behavior for grammar-constrained decoding. 3) This defensive pattern should be applied to any attribute that may differ between CUDA and Ascend SchedulerOutput implementations.
name: vllm-0.11.2-mrope-get-input-positions-tensor-removal
